steps.title: Analyze $f(x)=-6(3)^x-3$
steps.content: The base $3>1$ makes $(3)^x$ grow without bound as $x\to\infty$, and the $-6$ coefficient flips the sign, so $-6(3)^x\to-\infty$. Thus $f(x)\to-\infty$ as $x\to\infty$, meaning $f$ has no minimum value. The horizontal asymptote $y=-3$ is approached as $x\to-\infty$ but never attained.

steps.title: Analyze $g(x)=-3(6)^x$
steps.content: Similarly $(6)^x\to\infty$ as $x\to\infty$, so $g(x)=-3(6)^x\to-\infty$. There is no minimum value; the asymptote $y=0$ is approached as $x\to-\infty$ but never reached, and $g$ never equals $-3$ at a minimum.

steps.title: Choose the statement
steps.content: Neither function has a minimum value of $-3$: both continue decreasing without bound. That matches choice D.
